Camille K. Fong, Esq., of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, (415-362-2580) for defendant / moving party American Honda Motor Co., Inc. (See party's motion to dismiss or stay for non conveniens)
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Donald Silver, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO STAY FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion to stay the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is granted. Defendant successfully demonstrated that California is a seriously inconvenient forum as the balance of private and public interest factors makes it just that the litigation proceed in Oregon. The action is stayed until such time as plaintiff has obtained jurisdiction over all defendants in Oregon and, at that time, the case will be dismissed. If, after filing in Oregon, plaintiff can conclusively show to this court that certain defendants are not subject to personal jurisdiction in Oregon, the court will entertain a request to lift the stay. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 = (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Feb-23-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Camille K. Fong, Esq., of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, (415-362-2580) for defendant / moving party American Honda Motor Co., Inc.
J. Paul Fanning, Esq., of LANKFORD CRAWFORD MORENO LLP (925-300-3520), appearing for defendant / moving party Ford Motor Company (See party's Motion to stay for Forum Non Conveniens)
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Donald Silver, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant American Honda Motor Co.'s motion to dismiss the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Oregon is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all remaining defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) Defendant's alternate motion to stay the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is granted. Defendant successfully demonstrated that California is a seriously inconvenient forum as the balance of private and public interest factors makes it just that the litigation proceed in Oregon. The action is stayed until such time as plaintiff has obtained jurisdiction over all defendants in Oregon and, at that time, the case will be dismissed. If, after filing in Oregon, plaintiff can conclusively show to this court that certain defendants are not subject to personal jurisdiction in Oregon, the court will entertain a request to lift the stay. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 = (503/TLJ)
